three_body.instability_note: Moderate conflict between the three signals (35-pt spread). ESG score reads better than sector peer rank implies. The FTSE 100's overweight in energy and mining inflates relative ESG rankings.
three_body.resolution: Our resolution: Integrity 77/100 × WEM 5/100 → 'Engagement Priority'. Jangani, Date & Tucker (SSRN 5618192, 2026) show driver rankings shift across regime windows (COVID → COP26 → energy crisis). We anchor to verifiable harm metrics (emissions, pay ratios, enforcement records) that are regime-stable — not ESG labels that oscillate with market narrative.

summary_investor: ExxonMobil Corporation is projected to hold steady in its energy peer ranking (5→5 of 5) over 3 months. FTSE 100 position: 2nd→2nd percentile. WEM 5/100 — material ESG risk — recommend underweight or significant risk premium. Placebo Index 0.80 — ESG narrative overstates real performance.
summary_corporate: 12-month sector ranking forecast: hold steady (5→5 of 5). FTSE 100: 2nd→2nd percentile. Highest-leverage action: Physical Impacts of Climate Change (76% materiality) — this single driver has the greatest impact on FTSE 100 ranking improvement. ESG scores overstate performance by 31 pts — close this gap by improving Physical Impacts of Climate Change disclosure quality.
summary_auditor: Of 2 ESG claims analysed, 0 (0%) are independently verifiable. No directly contradicted claims — disclosure quality above sector average. ESG provider spread: 2 pts — within normal range. Priority: third-party audit of Physical Impacts of Climate Change measurement methodology.
crowd_consensus: Aggregated signal across investor, corporate, and audit lenses: Lagging — underweight; high harm + ESG overstatement confirmed. Driver rankings are temporally unstable across market regimes (Jangani et al., SSRN 5618192). The 3-body instability — ESG score, peer rank, FTSE position pulling in different directions — is resolved by WEM (5/100) × Integrity (77/100): regime-stable signals grounded in verifiable harm, not rater methodology.
